title: Compose Development, Overrides, Profiles, Watch, and Test Workflows · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Compose Run Exec Test
summary: Developers need fast source sync and optional debugging while CI needs isolated dependencies, deterministic tests, and no production-only side effects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose run exec test increment in development and test Compose workflow matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose run, exec, up, wait, exit-code-from, abort-on-exit, attach, and logs for repeatable test and migration jobs.
Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example override bind-mounts production paths, watch rebuilds on generated files, and dev and test profiles silently change dependencies. Render every file combination and isolate disposable state. Competency probe: Choose run, exec, up, wait, exit-code-from, abort-on-exit, attach, and logs for repeatable test and migration jobs. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Run Exec Test, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to development and test Compose workflow matrix.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-compose-run-exec-test — Choose run, exec, up, wait, exit-code-from, abort-on-exit, attach, and logs for repeatable test and migration jobs.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-compose-development-testing-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-compose-development-testing-3
    artifact: compose.dev.yaml
    decision: worked docker compose development testing 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      develop:
      watch:
      - action: sync
      path: ./src
      profiles: [dev]
      test:
      profiles: [test]
    verify: docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.dev.yaml config --profiles
    failure: Reject production bind mounts, hidden override precedence, watch-triggered dependency churn, or a profile that changes the core dependency contract.
    repair: Render every file combination, scope development-only state, select sync versus rebuild deliberately, and prove test teardown is isolated.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: docker compose run and exec create and target the same process and container state. This fails the Docker Compose Run Exec Test contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
